name: Chile De Arbol Salsa
recipeIngredient: 1/2 bunch Cilantro, leaves only, roughly chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the broiler.
2. Place the tomatoes and tomatillos on a baking sheet.
3. Broil, turning occasionally, until charred all over, about 10 to 12 minutes.
4. Transfer the tomatoes and tomatillos to a saucepan along with the remaining ingredients.
5. Bring to a boil and cook until the onions are soft, 12 to 15 minutes.
6. Transfer the mixture to a food processor or blender.
7. Puree the mixture, then strain it through a fine-mesh sieve.
8. Serve at room temperature or slightly chilled.
9. Chile De Arbol salsa can be stored in the refrigerator for 3 to 5 days or frozen for weeks.
